title: Convergence of a Regular Stochastic Matrix
description: A diagram showing how repeated application of a regular stochastic matrix P on an initial state vector leads to a unique steady-state vector, independent of the starting state.
elements: State Vector after one step v1 = P * v0
State Vector after n steps vn = P^n * v0
Unique Steady-State Vector π (where π = P * π)
relationships: Matrix P is applied repeatedly to transform each state vector into the next
After sufficient steps (n -> ∞), vn converges to π
The steady-state vector π is an eigenvector of P with eigenvalue 1
